questions.original_question: 路特斯科技的欧洲、中国、美国分别的门店数量占比如何？
questions.answer: 2024年，路特斯科技在全球的门店总数为208家，其中欧洲、中国和美国是其重点市场。具体来看，欧洲作为路特斯品牌的发源地，拥有70家门店，占全球门店总数的34%，体现了其在欧洲市场的持续扩展和优化。中国市场的门店数量为50家，占比约为24%，显示出路特斯科技在中国市场的积极布局。与此同时，美国市场拥有49家门店，占比约23.6%，表明路特斯科技在美国的稳步扩展为其全球增长提供了重要支持。总体而言，这三个地区共同构成了路特斯科技全球门店网络的核心部分。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

rag_info.chunk_content: "Lotus Technology continued to deliver meaningful operational growth amid global trade uncertainties, with total deliveries of 12,134 units in 2024, up 74% year-on-year, outperforming the broad luxury market,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. “Lotus Technology has proactively adapted its strategies by expanding its product pricing range and introducing new configurations to meet diversified market needs and broaden its customer base, targeting to keep global delivery growth ahead of the broad luxury market. At the same time, Lotus Technology is actively exploring potential strategic responses to trade uncertainties to keep expanding in key global markets, such as North America, where the company has long been committed. Through the ONE LOTUS strategy, Lotus Technology is fully integrating all businesses under the Lotus brand, which will strengthen brand equity and enhance operational flexibility and internal synergies. Lotus Technology is confident in creating substantial long-term value for its customers and shareholders.” Dr. Daxue Wang, Chief Financial Officer, said: "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements, streamlined operations to drive efficiency, optimized resource allocation, and reduced operating expenses for five consecutive quarters. Though the company’s gross margin was lower to 3% in 2024 due to tariff impact and proactive destock in response to the global trade uncertainties, Lotus Technology has initiated strategic cost optimization to improve profitability. As Lotus Technology progresses with the acquisition of Lotus UK, the company is committed to driving cost streamlining and operational enhancements across all markets to continuously deliver long-term value."

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports cars for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for
the Full Year of 2024 
summary: In 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable total of 12,134 vehicle deliveries, reflecting a significant 74% increase from the previous year. This growth was bolstered by an extensive network of over 200 stores globally, enabling the Company to navigate challenges posed by global trade uncertainties and economic slowdowns. The European market emerged as the largest contributor, accounting for nearly 40% of total deliveries with a staggering year-on-year growth rate of 179%, driven by the successful launch of the Eletre and anticipated deliveries of the Emeya model.

China remained the second-largest market, contributing 25% to total deliveries despite facing heightened competition and economic challenges. North America also saw substantial growth, with the Emira sportscar model accounting for 21% of global deliveries as it commenced customer deliveries in the U.S. Additionally, the fourth quarter of 2024 marked the introduction of the Emeya model to several countries, including Malaysia and the UAE, leading to a 90% year-over-year growth in the "rest of the world" region, which represented 15% of total deliveries.

In terms of model performance, Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans saw deliveries rise to 6,862 units, a 57% increase from 2023, while sportscar deliveries surged by 102% to 5,272 units. Overall, the significant growth across various regions and model types underscores the Company's successful strategy and adaptability in a competitive market, providing valuable insights for future strategic decisions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

title: 4TH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2024 KEY NUMBERS 
summary: In 2024, Lotus achieved a remarkable 57% year-over-year increase in lifestyle vehicle deliveries, which constituted 57% of its total deliveries. The brand's home market, Europe, experienced exceptional growth of 179%, contributing nearly 40% to total deliveries. Despite increased competition and a challenging economic landscape, China remained a significant market, accounting for 25% of total deliveries. North America saw an extraordinary surge of 415% in deliveries, primarily driven by the launch of the Emira model, representing 21% of global deliveries. Additionally, the Rest of the World region contributed 15% to total deliveries, with nearly 90% year-on-year growth.

In terms of financial performance for FY2024, Lotus delivered 12,134 units, a 74% increase from 6,970 units in FY2023, with fourth-quarter deliveries rising by 20% to 4,506 units. Revenue for FY2024 reached $924 million, up 36% from $679 million in FY2023, although fourth-quarter revenue declined by 25% to $272 million. The cost of revenues increased significantly to $895 million, a 55% rise from the previous year, with fourth-quarter costs up 3% to $301 million.

Gross profit for FY2024 fell to $29 million, a 71% decrease, and the fourth quarter saw a gross loss of $29 million, contrasting with a profit in the same quarter the previous year. The gross profit margin dropped to 3% for FY2024, down from 15% in FY2023, with a negative margin of 11% in the fourth quarter. Operating losses for FY2024 reached $786 million, while the net loss nearly doubled in the fourth quarter to $441 million.

Overall, while Lotus demonstrated impressive growth in unit deliveries and revenue, the financial metrics revealed significant challenges, particularly in profitability, indicating a need for strategic adjustments moving forward.

title: FY2024 OUTLOOK 
summary: The summary highlights Lotus' strategic growth in global retail presence and market penetration as part of its Vision80 strategy, which focuses on enhancing distribution and transitioning to intelligent luxury electric vehicles. In 2023, Lotus operated 215 retail stores, with plans to increase this number to around 250 by the end of 2024 and over 300 by 2025, demonstrating significant expansion. 

Model-specific penetration rates show that the Emira (ICE Sportscar) was available in 90% of stores in 2023, with full coverage expected by 2024 and 2025. The Eletre (BEV E-SUV) started at a 60% penetration rate and is also projected to reach 100% by 2024 and 2025, reflecting a strong emphasis on battery electric vehicles. The Emeya (BEV E-Sedan), which was not available in 2023, is anticipated to penetrate 50% of retail markets in 2024, with complete availability expected by 2025.

Additionally, in 2024, Lotus celebrated Lotus Day with the launch of the limited-edition Evija Fittipaldi and showcased the Racecar Type 66, marking a significant milestone in its design history. The event also included the commencement of Emeya deliveries, which offer customers immersive experiences, including professional racer training and track racing opportunities. This comprehensive approach underscores Lotus' commitment to expanding its market presence and ensuring widespread availability of its innovative vehicle models.

The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,180 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Overview**

Lotus is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, renowned for its iconic British brand and over 70 years of racing heritage, which includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles. The company specializes in luxury lifestyle vehicles designed for daily use and has been positioned as a luxury lifestyle BEV maker since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017. This acquisition has provided significant support in manufacturing, supply chain management, and research and development.

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is anticipated to experience substantial growth, projected at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, reaching nearly 1.9 million units. Currently, the market is underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. As an early mover in this sector, Lotus aims to launch its E-segment BEV model ahead of competitors and achieve a 100% BEV portfolio by 2027.

The company’s first fully electric model, the Eletre, was launched in 2022 and features an 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture. The Eletre offers three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0-100 km/h acceleration in just 2.95 seconds. Deliveries for the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023, with plans for global expansion.

Following the Eletre, Lotus introduced its second vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Additionally, the company plans to launch two more BEV models: a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026. All BEV models are manufactured through a partnership with Geely Holding at a new facility in Wuhan, China, which has an annual production capacity of 150,000 units. This collaboration allows Lotus to scale operations and maintain flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, the company delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, including both lifestyle and sports cars, and employed 3,180 full-time staff, with 56.7% of the workforce dedicated to research and development. 

This strategic positioning and robust growth plan underscore Lotus's commitment to becoming a leader in the luxury BEV market while leveraging its rich heritage and innovative capabilities.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Permission, Review and Filing Required from the Authorities in Mainland China Relating to Overseas Offering 
summary: The regulatory landscape for China-based companies seeking to raise capital offshore has become increasingly stringent, reflecting the PRC government's intensified oversight. This trend was notably marked by the issuance of the "Opinions on Severely Cracking Down on Illegal Securities Activities" on July 6, 2021, which underscored the need for enhanced supervision of overseas listings. A key aspect of this oversight involves the establishment of regulatory systems aimed at mitigating risks associated with cybersecurity and data privacy.

Effective February 15, 2022, the revised Measures for Cybersecurity Review require online platform operators that process data impacting national security—specifically those with over one million users—to undergo a cybersecurity review prior to any overseas listing. This regulatory framework is part of a broader effort to ensure that companies comply with national security standards.

On February 17, 2023, the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) introduced the Overseas Listing Trial Measures, which mandate that companies, such as Lotus Tech, complete specific filing procedures before pursuing a listing on platforms like Nasdaq. Lotus Tech successfully submitted its filing, which was concluded on February 8, 2024. The Overseas Listing Filing Rules stipulate that issuers must file for various actions, including additional securities issuance and changes in listing status, and are required to report significant events within three business days.

As of the date of the prospectus, Lotus Tech has not encountered any cybersecurity reviews or sanctions from PRC authorities, aside from inquiries related to its filing. Legal counsel for Lotus Tech suggests that while the company believes it must file with the CSRC after issuing American Depositary Shares (ADSs), it does not anticipate needing a cybersecurity review for this specific offering. However, the evolving nature of regulatory interpretation and potential legal changes could result in the need for additional approvals or reviews.

Failure to secure the necessary permissions or misinterpretation of regulatory requirements could lead to significant costs for Lotus Tech and potential sanctions, adversely affecting its business operations, ability to attract foreign investment, and the overall value of its securities. For a comprehensive understanding of the regulatory complexities and their potential impacts on offshore offerings, stakeholders are advised to refer to the "Risk Factors" section of the documentation.

summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

Our product lineup includes a comprehensive range of Lotus-branded cars, reflecting our commitment to quality in the luxury automotive sector. In 2024, we achieved significant milestones, delivering 12,134 vehicles, including both lifestyle and sports models, through our distribution partner LTIL. Our workforce expanded to 1,996 full-time employees, with approximately 52% dedicated to Research and Development across teams in China and Europe. Financially, we experienced substantial revenue growth, with total revenues increasing from US$9.6 million in 2022 to US$924.3 million in 2024. However, this growth was accompanied by net losses, which escalated from US$724.6 million in 2022 to US$1,107.3 million in 2024.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: "Lotus Technology continued to deliver meaningful operational growth amid global trade uncertainties, with total deliveries of 12,134 units in 2024, up 74% year-on-year, outperforming the broad luxury market,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. “Lotus Technology has proactively adapted its strategies by expanding its product pricing range and introducing new configurations to meet diversified market needs and broaden its customer base, targeting to keep global delivery growth ahead of the broad luxury market. At the same time, Lotus Technology is actively exploring potential strategic responses to trade uncertainties to keep expanding in key global markets, such as North America, where the company has long been committed. Through the ONE LOTUS strategy, Lotus Technology is fully integrating all businesses under the Lotus brand, which will strengthen brand equity and enhance operational flexibility and internal synergies. Lotus Technology is confident in creating substantial long-term value for its customers and shareholders.” Dr. Daxue Wang, Chief Financial Officer, said: "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements, streamlined operations to drive efficiency, optimized resource allocation, and reduced operating expenses for five consecutive quarters. Though the company’s gross margin was lower to 3% in 2024 due to tariff impact and proactive destock in response to the global trade uncertainties, Lotus Technology has initiated strategic cost optimization to improve profitability. As Lotus Technology progresses with the acquisition of Lotus UK, the company is committed to driving cost streamlining and operational enhancements across all markets to continuously deliver long-term value."

title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

Lotus Technology maintained a growth trajectory amid global trade uncertainties and economic slowdown. The European market contributed nearly 40% of total deliveries. With the Eletre rollout and the Emeya commencing deliveries in mid-2024, the European market achieved a year-on-year growth rate of 179%, positioning Europe as Lotus Technology's largest global market in 2024. China, as the second largest market for Lotus Technology, contributed 25% to total deliveries despite intensifying market competition and economic downturn. In North America, the region contributed 21% of Lotus Technology's global deliveries as the Emira, the sportscar model produced in Hethel, UK, commenced customer deliveries in the U.S. The rest of the world region represented 15% of total deliveries with nearly 90% year-on-year growth. In the fourth quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology started to deliver the Emeya in Malaysia, Thailand, Singapore, the UAE, and the Philippines. Deliveries by Model Type

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for
the Full Year of 2024 
summary: In 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable total of 12,134 vehicle deliveries, reflecting a significant 74% increase from the previous year. This growth was bolstered by an extensive network of over 200 stores globally, enabling the Company to navigate challenges posed by global trade uncertainties and economic slowdowns. The European market emerged as the largest contributor, accounting for nearly 40% of total deliveries with a staggering year-on-year growth rate of 179%, driven by the successful launch of the Eletre and anticipated deliveries of the Emeya model.

China remained the second-largest market, contributing 25% to total deliveries despite facing heightened competition and economic challenges. North America also saw substantial growth, with the Emira sportscar model accounting for 21% of global deliveries as it commenced customer deliveries in the U.S. Additionally, the fourth quarter of 2024 marked the introduction of the Emeya model to several countries, including Malaysia and the UAE, leading to a 90% year-over-year growth in the "rest of the world" region, which represented 15% of total deliveries.

In terms of model performance, Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans saw deliveries rise to 6,862 units, a 57% increase from 2023, while sportscar deliveries surged by 102% to 5,272 units. Overall, the significant growth across various regions and model types underscores the Company's successful strategy and adaptability in a competitive market, providing valuable insights for future strategic decisions.

summary: The summary presents a comprehensive overview of Lotus Technology Inc.'s financial performance, focusing on revenue distribution by geographic regions and contract liabilities as of December 31 for the years 2024, 2023, and 2022.

In terms of revenue, the company reported significant growth in 2024, with total revenues reaching US$924,349 thousand, a substantial increase from US$679,008 thousand in 2023 and US$9,557 thousand in 2022. The geographic breakdown reveals notable fluctuations: revenue from the Chinese mainland decreased to US$314,368 thousand from US$419,448 thousand in 2023, while the UK experienced remarkable growth, soaring to US$208,255 thousand from US$83,827 thousand. Other regions, including Japan, Korea, and various European countries, also showed positive trends, contributing to the overall increase in revenue.

On the liability side, the company’s contract liabilities, which reflect unfulfilled financial commitments, totaled US$42,797 thousand at the end of 2024, down from US$50,430 thousand in 2023. Current liabilities from third-party contracts decreased, while those from related parties saw a significant rise. The report indicates that US$39,441 thousand of the contract liabilities recognized in 2024 were related to advance payments for vehicles or services, with expectations to recognize US$45,757 thousand as revenue within the next year.

Overall, the data highlights Lotus Technology Inc.'s evolving market presence, revenue generation capabilities across various regions, and its management of financial obligations, providing valuable insights into the company's operational dynamics and financial health.

The remaining US$44,847 will be recognized over the remaining contract periods of 1 to 10 years. Lotus Technology Inc. has elected, as a practical expedient, not to disclose the transaction price allocated to unsatisfied or partially unsatisfied performance obligations that are part of a contract that has an original expected duration of one year or less.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,180 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,180 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Ten Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited
Second Quarter and First Half 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. reported its strongest performance to date, bolstered by a well-balanced global allocation and significant deliveries in the US following its market re-entry. The company anticipates that the introduction of bespoke services and growth in technology-related income will further enhance profit margins. However, it has adjusted its annual delivery target due to short-term challenges related to trade uncertainties and tariff increases. Additionally, the brand has initiated the "Win26" Plan, which aims to achieve positive operating cash flow and EBITDA by 2026. The unaudited financial results for the second quarter and the first half of 2024, ending June 30, were disclosed in a report released on August 28, 2024, highlighting Lotus's position as a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions.

title: FY2024 OUTLOOK 
summary: The summary highlights Lotus' strategic growth in global retail presence and market penetration as part of its Vision80 strategy, which focuses on enhancing distribution and transitioning to intelligent luxury electric vehicles. In 2023, Lotus operated 215 retail stores, with plans to increase this number to around 250 by the end of 2024 and over 300 by 2025, demonstrating significant expansion. 

Model-specific penetration rates show that the Emira (ICE Sportscar) was available in 90% of stores in 2023, with full coverage expected by 2024 and 2025. China has responded by imposing, and proposing to impose additional, new, or higher tariffs on certain products imported from the United States. On January 15, 2020, the Economic and Trade Agreement between the United States of America and the People’s Republic of China was entered into as a phase one trade deal, effective on February 14, 2020. On May 14, 2024, the U.S. government announced higher tariffs on steel and aluminum, semiconductors, electric vehicles, batteries, critical minerals, solar cells, ship-to-shore cranes, and medical products. These higher tariffs were based on claims that China has engaged in unfair trade practices. The highest of these tariffs are applicable to electric vehicles, which will be subject to a tariff rate of 100% from August 1, 2024, an increase from the earlier rate of 25%. From August 21, 2024, the European Commission imposed higher tariffs on imports of electric vehicles made in China. These new tariffs, which apply across the European Union, range from 17.0% to 36.3%, depending on the original equipment manufacturer that produced the vehicle. Shortly after these higher EU tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles were confirmed by a majority of EU states, China imposed provisional tariffs on brandy imports from the United States, with rates of 30.6% or 39%, effective October 11, 2024.

In retaliation, China has enacted its own tariffs on U.S. imports, further escalating trade tensions. The European Commission has also introduced higher tariffs on Chinese-made EVs, compounding the challenges for the company. These tariffs and trade disputes could adversely affect demand for its vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), hinder competitive positioning, and disrupt research and development efforts.

Additionally, the U.S. government has tightened export controls, particularly concerning military-related technologies and investments in sectors such as advanced microchips and artificial intelligence, targeting entities linked to China. This regulatory scrutiny may limit the company's ability to attract capital from U.S. investors and complicate cross-border collaborations.

The geopolitical landscape remains uncertain, with ongoing conflicts potentially leading to further sanctions and economic disruptions. While the company does not have direct operations in these conflict zones, the broader implications of strained international relations could negatively impact its business operations and financial performance. Overall, the combination of escalating tariffs, stringent export controls, and geopolitical tensions poses significant risks to the company's future prospects and operational stability.

The rights of Lotus Technology's shareholders and the fiduciary duties of Lotus Technology's directors under Cayman Islands law are different from what they would be under statutes or judicial precedent in some jurisdictions in the United States. In particular, the Cayman Islands has a different body of securities laws than the United States, and some U.S. states, such as Delaware, may have more fully developed and judicially interpreted bodies of corporate law than the Cayman Islands. In addition, shareholders of Cayman Islands companies may not have standing to initiate a shareholder derivative action in a federal court of the United States. Shareholders of Cayman Islands exempted companies like Lotus Technology have no general rights under Cayman Islands law to inspect corporate records (other than the memorandum and articles of association, special resolutions, and the register of mortgages and charges, of such companies) or to obtain copies of lists of shareholders of these companies. The directors of Lotus Technology have discretion under the post-offering articles of association that will become effective immediately prior to completion of the Business Combination to determine whether or not, and under what conditions, the corporate records may be inspected by the shareholders, but are not obliged to make them available to the shareholders.

In contrast, U.S. corporate governance is shaped by a robust legal framework that includes state-specific statutes and federal securities laws, enforced by agencies like the SEC. This framework emphasizes a balance between management authority and shareholder rights, detailing voting rights, access to information, and mechanisms for shareholder activism. title: ENFORCEABILITY OF CIVIL LIABILITY 
summary: LTC, a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, faces significant challenges regarding legal proceedings in the United States due to its offshore status and the location of most of its assets. This complicates the service of legal documents and the enforcement of U.S. court judgments, particularly those based on U.S. securities laws, which may not be recognized by Cayman Islands courts. While there is a possibility for U.S. judgments to be enforced under Cayman common law, specific conditions must be met, and judgments imposing punitive obligations are unlikely to be upheld. Additionally, LTC has received legal guidance indicating that the recognition and enforcement of U.S. court judgments in mainland China are also uncertain, as Chinese courts may refuse to acknowledge such judgments if they conflict with local legal principles or threaten national interests. Currently, there is no treaty or reciprocity agreement between mainland China and the U.S. or the Cayman Islands that facilitates the enforcement of foreign judgments, leaving the criteria for such enforcement ambiguous.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,180 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.
